title: SACRAMENTO

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Recent actions surrounding Roe v. Wade and the Supreme Court have put more than 36 million Americans at risk of losing access to abortion care.",
    "California could see an increase in out-of-state patients from 46,000 to 1.4 million, representing a nearly 3,000 percent increase.",
    "AB 1918 aims to support and expand the reproductive health care workforce in California.",
    "The California Reproductive Health Service Corps will be responsible for recruiting, training, and retaining a culturally and linguistically diverse workforce of health care professionals.",
    "In California, 40% of counties do not have an abortion provider, and nine counties do not have a single OB-GYN.",
    "AB 1918 will fund training necessary to expand access to reproductive care in underserved areas of California.",
    "If Roe v. Wade is overturned, people across the country will travel to California to access reproductive health care.",
    "It is critical for California to increase the network of clinicians trained to provide abortion services."
  ]
}
```
